{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the optimization problem\n\\[\n\\begin{array}{ll}\n\\text{minimize} & x^2 \\\\\n\\text{subject to} & c(x)=0,\n\\end{array}\n\\qquad x\\in \\mathbf{R},\n\\]\nwhere\n\\[\nc(x)=\n\\begin{cases}\nx^6\\sin(1/x) & \\text{if } x\\neq 0,\\\\\n0 & \\text{if } x=0.\n\\end{cases}\n\\]\nShow that the constraint function \(c\) is twice continuously differentiable for all \(x\\in \\mathbf{R}\), including at \(x=0\), and that the feasible points are exactly \(x=0\) and \(x=1/(k\\pi)\) for all nonzero integers \(k\)."}
